item.title: Speeding up GPU kernels by 38% with a multi-agent system
Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Problem, Dark DNA Unveiled, Pitfalls for Assistive Models, Simulating Fluid Dynamics
Dagster 1.13: Octopus's Garden
item.description: Dagster skills, partitioned asset checks, state backed components, virtual assets, and stronger integrations.

item.title: Bugbot now self-improves with learned rules
Introducing Adaptive: a smarter way to use Windsurf
item.description: We're launching multiple updates to Windsurf today: an Adaptive model router, a redesigned model picker with pricing context, and the removal of daily limits for Max.

item.title: Better MoE model inference with warp decode
Claude Code’s Source Leaks, OpenAI Exits Video Generation, Gemini Adds Music Generation, LLMs Learn at Inference
Monorepos, the hub-and-spoke model, and Copybara
item.description: How we configure Copybara for bi-directional syncing to enable a hub-and-spoke model for Git repositories

item.title: Meet the new Cursor
item.description: Cursor 3 is a unified workspace for building software with agents.

item.title: Making Dagster Easier to Contribute to in an AI-Driven World
item.description: AI has made contributing to open source easier but reviewing contributions is still hard. At Dagster, we’re improving the contributor experience with smarter review tooling, clearer guidelines, and a focus on contributions that are easier to evaluate, merge, and maintain.

item.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
item.description: Today, we're previewing the fastest way to run Ollama on Apple silicon, powered by MLX, Apple's machine learning framework.

item.title: Nvidia’s Open Salvo, OpenAI’s Amazon Deal, Grok Cuts Video Prices, Recursive Language Models
The Revenge of the Data Scientist
Run cloud agents in your own infrastructure
Fast regex search: indexing text for agent tools
Attacks On Data Centers, Qwen3.5 In All Sizes, DeepSeek’s Huawei Play, Apple’s Multimodal Tokenizer
Introducing Composer 2
item.description: Frontier-level coding with strong CursorBench results, higher token efficiency, and a faster default variant.

item.title: Introducing our new Windsurf pricing plans
item.description: We're simplifying Windsurf pricing across Free, Pro, and Teams alongside launching a new Max plan for our power users. The new plans replace the current credit-based system with industry-standard quotas.

item.title: DataOps with Dagster: A Practical Guide to Building a Reliable Data Platform
item.description: DataOps is about building a system that provides visibility into what's happening and control over how it behaves

item.title: Securing our codebase with autonomous agents
GPT-5.4 Makes A Splash, AI’s Growth on Mobile, Data Centers Go Off-Grid, Apple’s Diffusion Research
Unlocking the Full Value of Your Databricks
item.description: Standardizing on Databricks is a smart strategic move, but consolidation alone does not create a working operating model across teams, tools, and downstream systems. By pairing Databricks and Unity Catalog with Dagster, enterprises can add the coordination layer needed for dependency visibility, end-to-end lineage, and faster, more confident delivery at scale.

item.title: How we compare model quality in Cursor
item.description: We use a hybrid online-offline eval process to keep our understanding of model quality aligned with what developers actually do.

item.title: Anthropic vs. the U.S. Government, Nano Banana’s Makeover, Frontier Agent Management, Google’s Mathematics Solutions
GPT-5.4 is now available in Windsurf
item.description: GPT-5.4 is now available in Windsurf with multiple reasoning effort levels. For a limited time, self serve users enjoy promotional pricing starting at 1x credits.

item.title: Announcing AI Driven Data Engineering
item.description: AI coding agents are changing how data engineers work. This Dagster University course shows how to build a production-ready ELT pipeline from prompts while learning practical patterns for reliable AI-assisted development.

item.title: PlanetScale protects production reliability with Bugbot
Gemini Seizes the Lead, Investors Panic Over Agentic AI, Optimism at Global AI Summit, Local Versus Cloud
The third era of AI software development
item.description: A third era of AI software development is emerging as autonomous cloud agents take on larger tasks over longer timescales.

item.title: When to Move from Dagster OSS to Dagster+
item.description: Dagster OSS is built for builders. But as teams grow, the operational burden of running the platform can quietly consume engineering time. This guide explains when it makes sense to move to Dagster+ and shift your focus back to building data products.

item.title: The simplest and fastest way to setup OpenClaw
item.description: Setup OpenClaw in under two minutes with a single Ollama command.

item.title: The New Open-Weights Leader, Big AI’s Political Influence, Predicting Illness, Faster Reasoning
Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in Windsurf
item.description: Gemini 3.1 Pro is now available in Windsurf with Low and High thinking variants. For a limited time, enjoy promotional pricing on credit usage.

item.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available in Windsurf
item.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available in Windsurf with limited-time promotional pricing for self serve users: 2x credits without thinking and 3x credits with thinking.

item.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
item.description: Ollama now supports subagents and web search in Claude Code.

item.title: GLM-5 and Minimax M2.5 are now available in Windsurf
item.description: GLM-5 from Zhipu AI and Minimax M2.5 are now available in Windsurf with limited-time promotional pricing. Both models are included in Arena Mode's Frontier Arena and Hybrid Arena battle groups.

item.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Thinks Smarter, xAI Joins SpaceX, AI Outperforms Doctors, Standardized AI Audits
Box chooses Cursor for enterprise-grade quality, security, and control
Sample-Level Versioning for ML Pipelines with Dagster and Metaxy
item.description: Learn how Metaxy can be used to build multimodal data pipelines with sample-level granularity on Dagster

item.title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark is now available in Windsurf
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, an ultra-fast model optimized for real-time coding, is now available in Windsurf's Arena Mode Fast and Hybrid battle groups.

item.title: OpenClaw Runs Amok, Kimi’s Open Model, Ministral Distilled, Wikipedia’s Partners
NVIDIA commits 3x more code across 30,000 developers with Cursor
Evaluating Skills
item.description: We built a light weight evaluation framework to quantiatively measure the effectiveness of the Dagster Skills, and these are our findings.

item.title: Great Infrastructure Needs Great Stories: Designing our Children’s Book
item.description: We set out to explain Dagster assets in the simplest possible way: as living characters that wait, react, and change with their dependencies. By designing a children’s book with warmth, visuals, and motion, we rediscovered what makes assets compelling in the first place.

item.title: Closing the DataOps Loop: Why We Built Compass for Dagster+
item.description: Detection isn't the bottleneck anymore. Understanding is. Compass closes the loop by turning Dagster+ operational data into a conversation.

item.title: OpenClaw
item.description: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant that connects your messaging apps to local AI coding agents, all running on your own device.

item.title: Agents Go Shopping, Intelligence Redefined, Better Text in Pictures, Higher Engagement Means Worse Alignment
Pytest for Agent-Generated Code: Concrete Testing Strategies to Put Into Practice
item.description: When agents write tests, intent matters as much as correctness. By defining clear testing levels, preferred patterns, and explicit anti-patterns, we give agents the structure they need to produce fast, reliable Pytest suites that scale with automation.

item.title: ollama launch
item.description: ollama launch is a new command which sets up and runs coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex with local or cloud models. No environment variables or config files needed.

item.title: Self-Driving Reasoning Models, ChatGPT Adds Ads, Apple’s Deal with Google, 3D Generation Pronto
Salesforce accelerates velocity by over 30% and ships higher-quality code with Cursor
Dagster + Snowflake: Building Production AI Pipelines with Cortex
item.description: Snowflake handles AI compute while Dagster handles orchestration, observability, and the operational patterns that turn AI experiments into reliable production pipelines.

item.title: Image generation (experimental)
item.description: Generate images locally with Ollama on macOS. Windows and Linux support coming soon.

item.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
item.description: Ollama is now compatible with the Anthropic Messages API, making it possible to use tools like Claude Code with open models.

item.title: Governments vs. Grok, Meta Buys Agent Tech, Healthcare Chatbots, Limits of AI-Powered Retrieval
LLM Evals: Everything You Need to Know
OpenAI Codex with Ollama
item.description: Open models can be used with OpenAI's Codex CLI through Ollama. Codex can read, modify, and execute code in your working directory using models such as gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b, or other open-weight alternatives.

item.title: LLMs Go To Confession, Automated Scientific Research, What Copilot Users Want, Reasoning For Less
New Year Special! Hopes for 2026 from David Cox, Adji Bousso Dieng, Juan M. Lavista Ferres, Tanmay Gupta, Pengtao Xie, Sharon Zhou
Top Stories of 2025! Big AI Poaches Talent, Reasoning Models Boost Performance, Agents Write Code, Data Centers Drive GDP, China Turns the Tables
OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
item.description: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to bring the latest gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models to users for safety classification tasks. gpt-oss-safeguard models are available in two sizes: 20B and 120B, and are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

item.title: MiniMax M2
item.description: MiniMax M2 is now available on Ollama's cloud. It's a model built for coding and agentic workflows.

item.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark performance
item.description: We ran performance tests on release day firmware and an updated Ollama version to see how Ollama performs.

item.title: New coding models & integrations
item.description: GLM-4.6 and Qwen3-coder-480B are available on Ollama’s cloud service with easy integrations to the tools you are familiar with. Qwen3-Coder-30B has been updated for faster, more reliable tool calling in Ollama’s new engine.

item.title: Qwen3-VL
item.description: Ollama now supports Alibaba's Qwen3-VL.

item.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark
item.description: The latest NVIDIA DGX Spark is here! Ollama has partnered with NVIDIA to ensure it runs fast and efficiently out-of-the-box.

item.title: Selecting The Right AI Evals Tool
Web search
item.description: A new web search API is now available in Ollama. Ollama provides a generous free tier of web searches for individuals to use, and higher rate limits are available via Ollama’s cloud.

item.title: New model scheduling
item.description: Ollama now includes a significantly improved model scheduling system, reducing crashes due to out of memory issues, maximizing GPU utilization and performance, especially on multi-GPU systems.

item.title: Cloud models
item.description: Cloud models are now in preview, letting you run larger models with fast, datacenter-grade hardware. You can keep using your local tools while running larger models that wouldn’t fit on a personal computer.

item.title: OpenAI gpt-oss
item.description: Ollama partners with OpenAI to bring gpt-oss to Ollama and its community.

item.title: Ollama's new app
item.description: Ollama's new app is now available for macOS and Windows.

item.title: Inspect AI, An OSS Python Library For LLM Evals
Golden Ages
item.description: Every industry has a golden era. And once it ends, it is impossible to break through. Why do Golden Ages begin, and why do they end?

item.title: Secure Minions: private collaboration between Ollama and frontier models
item.description: Secure Minions is a secure protocol built by Stanford's Hazy Research lab to allow encrypted local-remote communication.

item.title: Thinking
item.description: Ollama now has the ability to enable or disable thinking. This gives users the flexibility to choose the model’s thinking behavior for different applications and use cases.

item.title: Streaming responses with tool calling
item.description: Ollama now supports streaming responses with tool calling. This enables all chat applications to stream content and also call tools in real time.

item.title: Ollama's new engine for multimodal models
item.description: Ollama now supports new multimodal models with its new engine.

item.title: A Field Guide to Rapidly Improving AI Products
Minions: where local and cloud LLMs meet
item.description: Avanika Narayan, Dan Biderman, and Sabri Eyuboglu from Christopher Ré's Stanford Hazy Research lab, along with Avner May, Scott Linderman, James Zou, have developed a way to shift a substantial portion of LLM workloads to consumer devices by having small on-device models (such as Llama 3.2 with Ollama) collaborate with larger models in the cloud (such as GPT-4o).

item.title: Everything You Call Life
item.description: "Life can be much broader, once you discover one simple fact, and that is that everything around you that you call ‘life’ was made up by people that were no smarter than you. And you can change it, you can influence it, you can build your own things that other people can use." - Steve Jobs

item.title: Core Values
item.description: Institutions rot. Empires decay. And companies that once seemed like behemoths wither and die. The average age of a company in the S&P 500 is only 21 years. But why is this?

item.title: Reinforcement Learning Heats Up, White House Orders Muscular AI Policy, Computer Use Gains Momentum, Fine Control of Fine-Tuning
DeepSeek Sharpens Its Reasoning: DeepSeek-R1, an affordable rival to OpenAI’s o1
Thoughts On A Month With Devin
How London Lost Fashion
item.description: London was the epicenter of fashion nearly all of history. In one century, Paris usurped the title. How did this come to be, and what lessons can America apply today?

item.title: nbsanity - Share Notebooks as Polished Web Pages in Seconds
Structured outputs
item.description: Ollama now supports structured outputs making it possible to constrain a model's output to a specific format defined by a JSON schema. The Ollama Python and JavaScript libraries have been updated to support structured outputs.

item.title: Building an Audience Through Technical Writing: Strategies and Mistakes
Ollama Python library 0.4 with function calling improvements
item.description: With Ollama Python library version 0.4, functions can now be provided as tools. The library now also has full typing support and new examples have been added.

item.title: Why Are Startups Hard?
item.description: It's not the hours - many jobs require insane hours. It's not the stress - there are many stressful jobs. So what makes startups so uniquely challenging?

item.title: Llama 3.2 Vision
item.description: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B and 90B models are now available in Ollama.

item.title: Using LLM-as-a-Judge For Evaluation: A Complete Guide
IBM Granite 3.0 models
item.description: Ollama partners with IBM to bring Granite 3.0 models to Ollama.

item.title: Product Market Fit
item.description: If your company cannot withstand a competitor giving away your product FOR FREE, you do not have product market fit.

item.title: Llama 3.2 goes small and multimodal
item.description: Ollama partners with Meta to bring Llama 3.2 to Ollama.

item.title: Reduce hallucinations with Bespoke-Minicheck
item.description: Bespoke-Minicheck is a new grounded factuality checking model developed by Bespoke Labs that is now available in Ollama. It can fact-check responses generated by other models to detect and reduce hallucinations.

item.title: An Open Course on LLMs, Led by Practitioners
Tool support
item.description: Ollama now supports tool calling with popular models such as Llama 3.1. This enables a model to answer a given prompt using tool(s) it knows about, making it possible for models to perform more complex tasks or interact with the outside world.

item.title: Google Gemma 2
item.description: Gemma 2 is now available on Ollama in 3 sizes - 2B, 9B and 27B.

item.title: On Venting
item.description: Contrary to modern psychobabble, venting your feelings is not good for you. In fact, it is the greatest thing holding you back. Here is why I will never tolerate or listen to it.

item.title: What We’ve Learned From A Year of Building with LLMs
An entirely open-source AI code assistant inside your editor
item.description: Continue enables you to easily create your own coding assistant directly inside Visual Studio Code and JetBrains with open-source LLMs.

item.title: Google announces Firebase Genkit with Ollama support
item.description: At Google IO 2024, Google announced Ollama support in Firebase Genkit, a new open-source framework for developers to build, deploy and monitor production-ready AI-powered apps.

item.title: Llama 3 is not very censored
item.description: Compared to Llama 2, Llama 3 feels much less censored. Meta has substantially lowered false refusal rates. Llama 3 will refuse less than 1/3 of the prompts previously refused by Llama 2.

item.title: Llama 3
item.description: Llama 3 is now available to run on Ollama. This model is the next generation of Meta's state-of-the-art large language model, and is the most capable openly available LLM to date.

item.title: Embedding models
item.description: Embedding models are available in Ollama, making it easy to generate vector embeddings for use in search and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) applications.

item.title: Ollama now supports AMD graphics cards
item.description: Ollama now supports AMD graphics cards in preview on Windows and Linux. All the features of Ollama can now be accelerated by AMD graphics cards on Ollama for Linux and Windows.

item.title: Windows preview
item.description: Ollama is now available on Windows in preview, making it possible to pull, run and create large language models in a new native Windows experience. Ollama on Windows includes built-in GPU acceleration, access to the full model library, and serves the Ollama API including OpenAI compatibility.

item.title: Fuck You, Show Me The Prompt.
OpenAI compatibility
item.description: Ollama now has initial compatibility with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, making it possible to use existing tooling built for OpenAI with local models via Ollama.

item.title: Vision models
item.description: New vision models are now available: LLaVA 1.6, in 7B, 13B and 34B parameter sizes. These models support higher resolution images, improved text recognition and logical reasoning.

item.title: Python & JavaScript Libraries
item.description: The initial versions of the Ollama Python and JavaScript libraries are now available, making it easy to integrate your Python or JavaScript, or Typescript app with Ollama in a few lines of code. Both libraries include all the features of the Ollama REST API, are familiar in design, and compatible with new and previous versions of Ollama.

item.title: Dokku: my favorite personal serverless platform
Stop Thinking with IF Statements!
item.description: The most common mistake junior engineers make is a lack of systems thinking. And no where is this more obvious than their prolific use of if statements.

item.title: Building LLM-Powered Web Apps with Client-Side Technology
item.description: Recreate one of the most popular LangChain use-cases with open source, locally running software - a chain that performs Retrieval-Augmented Generation, or RAG for short, and allows you to “chat with your documents”

item.title: Ollama is now available as an official Docker image
item.description: Ollama can now run with Docker Desktop on the Mac, and run inside Docker containers with GPU acceleration on Linux.

item.title: Leveraging LLMs in your Obsidian Notes
item.description: This post walks through how you could incorporate a local LLM using Ollama in Obsidian, or potentially any note taking tool.

item.title: How to prompt Code Llama
item.description: This guide walks through the different ways to structure prompts for Code Llama and its different variations and features including instructions, code completion and fill-in-the-middle (FIM).

item.title: Run Code Llama locally
item.description: Meta's Code Llama is now available on Ollama to try.

item.title: Run Llama 2 uncensored locally
item.description: This post will give some example comparisons running Llama 2 uncensored model versus its censored model.

item.title: Why Should ML Engineers Learn Kubernetes?
nbdev + Quarto: A new secret weapon for productivity
ghapi, a new third-party Python client for the GitHub API
Nbdev: A literate programming environment that democratizes software engineering best practices
Data Science Meets Devops: MLOps with Jupyter, Git, & Kubernetes
GitHub Actions: Providing Data Scientists With New Superpowers.
Introducing fastpages, An easy to use blogging platform with extra features for Jupyter Notebooks.
CodeSearchNet Challenge: Evaluating the State of Semantic Code Search
How to Automate Tasks on GitHub With Machine Learning for Fun and Profit
How To Create Natural Language Semantic Search for Arbitrary Objects With Deep Learning
How To Create Magical Data Products Using Sequence-to-Sequence Models
How Docker Can Help You Become A More Effective Data Scientist
Automated Machine Learning — A Paradigm Shift That Accelerates Data Scientist Productivity @ Airbnb
How to Lose Time and Money
The Shape of the Essay Field
When To Do What You Love
Is There Such a Thing as Good Taste?
The Real Reason to End the Death Penalty
The Bus Ticket Theory of Genius
How to Make Pittsburgh a Startup Hub
Why It's Safe for Founders to Be Nice
What Microsoft Is this the Altair Basic of?
Let the Other 95% of Great Programmers In
How to Be an Expert in a Changing World
The Top of My Todo List
What We Look for in Founders
The Top Idea in Your Mind
What Kate Saw in Silicon Valley
Why Twitter is a Big Deal
How to Be an Angel Investor
Can You Buy a Silicon Valley? Maybe.
What I've Learned from Hacker News
Could VC be a Casualty of the Recession?
Why to Start a Startup in a Bad Economy
You Weren't Meant to Have a Boss
Six Principles for Making New Things
Why to Move to a Startup Hub
Holding a Program in One's Head
Why to Not Not Start a Startup
The Hardest Lessons for Startups to Learn
How to Do What You Love
What Business Can Learn from Open Source
Why Smart People Have Bad Ideas
A Unified Theory of VC Suckage
This Year We Can End the Death Penalty in California
GDP.pdf: Can $100B AI Models Master the Documents that Run the World?
item.description: Can frontier models master the documents that run the world? GDP.pdf is a multimodal and reasoning benchmark that takes real-world prompts and PDFs pulled directly from expert professional workflows.

item.title: Riemann-bench: A Benchmark for Moonshot Mathematics
item.description: Riemann-bench is a verifiable benchmark of extreme-tier mathematical problems where even frontier models score <10%.

item.title: EnterpriseBench: CoreCraft – Measuring AI Agents in Chaotic, Enterprise RL Environments
item.description: Stop testing models in tiny, self-contained environments. We built CoreCraft, a large-scale startup world, and deployed AI agents to solve real tasks. Our goal: to move agents beyond the cleanliness of the lab and into the chaos of enterprise reality.

item.title: Hemingway-bench Leaderboard: Because Good Writing Isn't a Checklist of Vibes
item.description: Stop rewarding slop. Hemingway-bench is an AI writing leaderboard that takes real-world writing tasks and puts them in front of master wordsmiths. Our goal: to push AI writing from two-second vibes to genuine nuance and impact.

item.title: Building AdvancedIF: Evolving Instruction Following Beyond IFEval and “Avoid the Letter C”
item.description: Meta Superintelligence Labs partnered with Surge to build AdvancedIF, an instruction-following benchmark where every prompt and rubric was written by human experts – not synthetically generated by an LLM. In instruction-following domains, where frontier models still fail 22-30%, using these human-crafted rubrics as reward signals for RL yields a 13% gain.

item.title: LMArena is a cancer on AI
item.description: Would you trust a medical system whose only metric was “which doctor wins the Internet?” No, you'd call that malpractice. Yet that's LMArena.

item.title: RL Environments and the Hierarchy of Agentic Capabilities
item.description: Our RL environment run on 9 models revealed the core capabilities all agents need to master: tool use, planning, adaptability, groundedness, and common sense.

item.title: How do frontier models perform on real-world finance problems?
item.description: We stress-tested GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude Sonnet 4.5 on 200+ expert finance tasks. Here's where even the best models break when they move from benchmarks to Wall Street.

item.title: A Product Take on Sonnet 4.5
item.description: After 100+ hours with Opus 4.1 and 20+ hours in the first week of Sonnet 4.5's launch, Nick Heiner, our VP of Product gives first impressions.

item.title: Is Sonnet 4.5 the best coding model in the world?
item.description: On Surge AI’s agentic coding benchmark, Claude Sonnet 4.5 outperformed GPT-5-Codex in accuracy, while GPT-5-Codex was more cost-efficient. Despite similar scores, the models were distinct in which tasks they failed in. In a refactoring case study, Claude succeeded after persistent debugging, while GPT-5-Codex failed due to an unexplained decision to end the task early. Both stayed focused and avoided hallucinations even when encountering difficulties.

item.title: The Human/AI Frontier: A Conversation with Bogdan Grechuk
item.description: At Surge AI, we work with the world’s sharpest minds to push the limits of AI. Professor Bogdan Grechuk – an IMO gold medalist and Associate Professor at the University of Leicester – is one of them. We interviewed him about the work he does to train SOTA models to perform frontier research.

item.title: SWE-Bench Failures: When Coding Agents Spiral Into 693 Lines of Hallucinations
item.description: When coding models spiral into self-reinforcing hallucinations, small mistakes compound into catastrophic failure. In SWE-bench, we saw SOTA models invent whole classes, methods, and terminal outputs – never realizing they had lost touch with the real codebase. In this case study, we’ll look at how three frontier coding agents tried to solve one particular SWE-bench problem: one spiraled into hallucinations and failed entirely, one spiraled but recovered, and one avoided hallucinations altogether. Our goal: to illustrate how dissecting real-world problems can steer models towards human-ready AGI.

item.title: Benchmarks are broken
item.description: Academic benchmarks make great headlines, and terrible AI.

item.title: Unsexy AI Failures: The PDF That Broke ChatGPT
item.description: The AI world loves climbing leaderboards. Companies race to hit #1 on LMSYS, chase perfect scores on academic benchmarks, and demo SVGs of pelicans on bicycles. These achievements make for great headlines and impressive presentations – even when these metrics are easily hacked.

item.title: Bringing light to the GPT-4o vs. GPT-5 personality controversy
item.description: GPT-5 was released on Aug 7, 2025. The swift removal of all legacy models from the ChatGPT UI was met with an even swifter backlash: some people online felt that GPT-4o was more personable, human, and engaging, whereas GPT-5 was stiff and robotic. This viral meme encapsulated the faction’s thesis:

item.title: DALL·E 3 and Midjourney Fail Astral Codex Ten's Image Generation Bet
item.description: An update on Astral Codex Ten's Image Generation Bet: close, but no dice. DALL·E 3 and Midjourney fail.

item.title: How Anthropic uses Surge AI to Train and Evaluate Claude
item.description: Learn how Anthropic partnered with Surge AI to gather high-quality human feedback at scale using the RLHF platform, resulting in one of the safest and most advanced large language models on the planet.

item.title: We Evaluated ChatGPT vs. Google on 500 Search Queries
item.description: We measured ChatGPT vs. Google on 500 search queries, and found that ChatGPT crushes Google on coding and ties it on general information — despite not being optimized for a search experience at all. Dive into this post to learn more about OpenAI’s existential threat to Google.

item.title: AI Red Teams for Adversarial Training: How to Make ChatGPT and LLMs Adversarially Robust
item.description: How do you make large language models safer and adversarially robust to counterattacks? Learn about AI red teams of creative data labelers who try to interactively penetrate AI defenses in order to teach them.

item.title: HellaSwag or HellaBad? 36% of this popular LLM benchmark contains errors
item.description: We analyzed HellaSwag, a popular LLM benchmark, and found errors in 36% of its rows.

item.title: How TikTok is Evolving the Next Generation of Search
item.description: TikTok has been taking over the world — and now, your Google Search results too. But when are they actually helpful? We ran a large-scale personalized human evaluation, asking Surgers to rate hundreds of <query, TikTok> pairs to find out.

item.title: Evaluating Generative AI: Did Astral Codex Ten Win His Bet on AI Progress?
item.description: Has Astral Codex Ten's bet on AI progress really been won? We asked Surgers to evaluate DALL·E and Imagen on Scott's 5 compositionality prompts!

item.title: Why Instagram is Losing Gen Z: We Asked 100 Users to Compare TikTok vs. Reels
item.description: Why can't Meta A/B test its way back to greatness? To move Instagram beyond short-term engagement metrics, we ran a personalized human evaluation asking 100 users to compare TikTok vs. Instagram Reels. Learn why Gen Z considers Reels the place where TikToks go to die, and what Instagram should do about it.

item.title: The $250K Inverse Scaling Prize and Human-AI Alignment
item.description: Surge AI is partnering with NYU and the Fund for Alignment Research on the Inverse Scaling Prize. If you've found a task with LLM inverse scaling properties, and need help creating a dataset of 300-500+ examples, reach out. We’re a human alignment platform with deep expertise in training large language models on human feedback, and we’re here to help – including $500 of free data labeling credits to kickstart your submission.

item.title: Search Behind-the-Scenes: How Neeva Uses Human Evaluation to Measure Search Quality
item.description: Search quality measurement is one of the trickiest, but most important parts of building Search. Read how Neeva uses human evaluation of search quality to build a state-of-the-art search engine challenging Google.

item.title: Human Evaluation of Large Language Models: How Good is Hugging Face’s BLOOM?
item.description: Hugging Face's BLOOM is a new 176B parameter multilingual large language model. How does it compare to other state-of-the-art LLMs? We ran a human evaluation across 7 real-world categories to evaluate its performance.

item.title: 30% of Google's Emotions Dataset is Mislabeled
item.description: Last year, Google released their “GoEmotions” dataset: a human-labeled dataset of 58K Reddit comments categorized according to 27 emotions. The problem? A whopping 30% of the dataset is mislabeled! Check out some of the egregious errors, and learn how to build better datasets.30% of Google's Emotions Dataset is Mislabeled

item.title: AI Red Teams and Adversarial Data Labeling with Redwood Research
item.description: Our mission at Surge AI is to inject human values and intelligence into AI. We want to build a world where AI

item.title: Humans vs. Gary Marcus vs. Slate Star Codex: When is an AI failure actually a failure?
item.description: Gary Marcus has several examples of AI mistakes. But are they really failures, or a sign of creativity? We gave them to 15 Surgers to complete GPT-3's "mistakes" to see how they would perform instead.

item.title: How Surge AI Built OpenAI's GSM8K Dataset of 8,500 Math Problems
item.description: We built a dataset of 8,500 Grade School Math Problems for OpenAI. The goal of the dataset: to train language models like GPT-3 to solve natural language math problems and measure their reasoning ability. Learn about our process in this blog post!

item.title: We asked 100 humans to draw the DALL·E prompts
item.description: Where do human artists fit in a world of rich, creative AI? We asked 100 Surgers to draw the DALL-E prompts.

item.title: Google Search is Falling Behind
item.description: Google Search is falling behind. We analyzed three areas – programming queries, sports queries, and cooking queries – to understand where Google Search lags behind its competitors.

item.title: Moving Beyond Engagement: Optimizing Facebook's Algorithms for Human Values
item.description: Social media platforms optimize for clicks and engagement — but those same short-term optimizations drive clickbait, toxic content, and misinformation. How can we align their ML systems to human values instead? This post describes a data-driven approach with Facebook.

item.title: Holy $#!t: Are popular toxicity models simply profanity detectors?
item.description: Are popular toxicity models simply profanity detectors? We show how toxicity models overweight profanity, and make mistakes when profanity is used in a positive way.

item.title: Is Google Search Deteriorating? Measuring Google's Search Quality in 2022
item.description: Has Google's Search Quality deteriorated in recent years? This post measures Google Search using human evaluation.

item.title: 5 Examples of the Importance of Context-Sensitivity in Data-Centric AI
item.description: Data-centric AI requires radically rethinking the data that goes into your models. Surge AI provides data labelers with the skills you need to get context-sensitive labels.

item.title: The AI Bottleneck: High-Quality, Human-Powered Data
item.description: In theory, AI has blown past our wildest dreams; in practice, Siri can’t even tell us the weather. The problem? Creating high-quality datasets to train and measure our models is still incredibly difficult. We should be able to gather 20,000 labels for training a Reddit classifier in a single
